exemplar.title:
What is the effect of different active ingredients (menthol, sodium
fluoride, zinc chloride, hexetidine and betaine) in mouth washes on the growth of bacteria
(non pathogenic Escherichia coli strain HB101) measured in the diameter of the inhibition
zone in a petri dish?

detailedExemplarData.title:
What is the effect of different active ingredients (menthol, sodium
fluoride, zinc chloride, hexetidine and betaine) in mouth washes on the growth of bacteria
(non pathogenic Escherichia coli strain HB101) measured in the diameter of the inhibition
zone in a petri dish?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a specific oralâhygiene context with clear linkage to inhibitionâzone measurements
Methodological considerations are explained (preliminary trial justification, control rationale, biosafety awareness)
Controlledâvariables table and preliminary experiment enhance experimental validity
weaknesses: Methodology description omits quantitative agar concentration and exact incubation temperature
Ambiguity in mouthâwash delivery method (disc vs. well) and lack of calibration details for equipment
Procedure could better link table identifiers to protocol steps to improve reproducibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands meet the 5â6 band (context and methodological considerations), but the methodology description falls into the 3â4 band due to minor ambiguities. Holistically this places Research Design in the upper part of the 3â4 band (score 4).

strengths: Data processing is appropriately and accurately executed (means, SD, ANOVA, Tukey) without arithmetic errors
Comprehensive rawâdata tables demonstrate meticulous recording
weaknesses: Communication of data recording/processing is not fully precise (generic graph labels, inconsistent table formatting)
Consideration of uncertainties is incompleteâuncertainty is calculated but not propagated into means or statistical tests
Inconsistent units (mm vs cm), rounding of p-values and typographical errors reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although data processing quality reaches the 5â6 band, communication and uncertainty strands remain in the 3â4 band with significant omissions. The overall performance best fits the lower half of the 3â4 band (score 3).

strengths: Conclusion clearly ranks mouthâwash effectiveness and references mean diameters and p-values
Justification of the main finding is consistent with the analysis presented
weaknesses: Comparison to the scientific context is superficial, lacking depth and critical engagement
Anomalous results (e.g., nearâzero inhibition by menthol) are not discussed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent and justified (5â6 band for relevance) but only makes a limited comparison to the scientific context (3â4 band). Holistically this aligns with the lower part of the 3â4 band (score 3).

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses and limitations are identified
Improvements suggested are realistic and linked to the stated weaknesses
weaknesses: The relative impact of each limitation is not deeply explained
Improvements are described but lack full explanation of how they mitigate the specific weaknesses
Some methodological factors (e.g., nonâactive ingredients) are omitted from the evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are in the 3â4 band (describes specific weaknesses and relevant improvements), but neither reaches the depth required for the upper half. Holistically this corresponds to a mid-band 3. Score distribution: 4 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 13.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion restates the ranked effectiveness and directly references mean diameters and p-values, demonstrating full consistency with analysis. Justification is explicit, so the strand sits in the 5-6 band; a â5â reflects a couple of phrasing inaccuracies but no substantive mismatch.

Raw-data tables and a bar graph with error bars are provided, and headings include units; however, table formatting is occasionally confusing and the graph axis labels are generic (âmean unitâ). Communication is **clear** enough to follow or copy, yet not consistently precise, placing it in the 3-4 band.

Means, standard deviations, one-way ANOVA and Tukey post-hoc tests are correctly applied and presented; raw data were processed without arithmetical errors. Despite rounding the ANOVA p-value to 0.00, the overall processing is **appropriate and accurate**, meriting the upper band though a perfect â6â is not awarded due to minor rounding issues.

Specific weaknesses such as uneven bacterial spreading, unknown active-ingredient concentration and non-circular zones are listed and their possible effects briefly described. Impact is mentioned but not **explained in relative depth**, aligning with the descriptor âdescribes specific weaknessesâ, band 3-4, lower mark.

The student cites two external studies comparing antimicrobial efficacy, but the discussion is brief and somewhat generic (âboth have significant antibacterial propertiesâ). This shows **some relevant comparison** yet lacks depth or critical linkage, fitting comfortably in band 3-4 at the lower mark.

The student states the research question clearly and embeds it in an explicitly oral-hygiene context, explaining why HB101 is chosen and how inhibition zones relate to mouth-wash effectiveness. The background on each active ingredient and its mode of action further narrows the context to a specific, appropriate biological framework. This exceeds a broad outline and fits the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate context.â

Instrument uncertainty (Â±0.1 cm) and percentage uncertainty are calculated, but propagation is not carried into means or statistical tests, and ruler precision is quoted in millimetres while raw data are centimetres. Evidence of consideration exists yet with **significant omissions and small inaccuracies**, fitting band 3-4 but nearer the lower mark.

Corresponding improvements (e.g., use paper discs, prepare pure active-ingredient solutions) are proposed and linked to each limitation, yet rationale remains brief. This meets the requirement for âdescribedâ rather than âexplainedâ improvements, placing it again in band 3-4 at a mid-range score.

Relevant methodological considerations are not merely listed but **explained**: preliminary trial justifies volume choice, controlled-variable table explains why each factor is controlled, and risk/ethical sections show awareness of biosafety. These explanations address sufficiency (five replicates, 48 h incubation) and relevance (diameter as outcome), satisfying the âexplainedâ requirement.

The step-by-step procedure with exact volumes, timings and replicate numbers would allow another student to reproduce the work, but there are **minor ambiguities**: agar concentration is not quantified, incubation temperature only stated as âroom temperatureâ, and mouth-wash drop delivery lacks clarification of disc or well. These small gaps match âfew ambiguitiesâ, so the upper band of 3-4 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The discussion of HB101âs probiotic role extends beyond the experimentâs scope; tightening the focus to oral hygiene applications of this strain would enhance relevance.
The hypothesis is clear and testable, but predicting only zinc chlorideâs effect limits scope; articulating expected rank order for all ingredients based on literature would strengthen rationale.
Linking mouthwash identifiers in Table 1 directly to list items in the procedure would reduce confusion and improve reproducibility of the methodology.
Units for measurement and uncertainty are inconsistently presented as mm and cm; adopt a single unit throughout to avoid conversion errors.
The controlled variables table is well structured; each factorâs rationale and control method are clearly articulated, strengthening experimental validity.
The student embeds the research question within a specific oral hygiene context, clearly linking inhibition zone measurements to antimicrobial efficacy, demonstrating strong contextual framing.
How might specifying additional metrics (e.g., inhibition zone area) refine the research question and provide deeper insight beyond diameter measurements?
Including a structural diagram of menthol when describing its antimicrobial mechanism could improve clarity and support the textâs technical detail.
Uncertainties are calculated but not propagated into the ANOVA or displayed as confidence intervals; including propagated error would strengthen data interpretation.
Present the percentage uncertainty formula as $$\frac{\text{absolute uncertainty}}{\text{mean}}\times100\%$$ for clearer mathematical communication.
The conclusion restates the rank order effectively but omits discussion on why menthol produced near-zero zones; addressing this anomaly would strengthen interpretation.
Incubation conditions (temperature and duration) are omitted from the controlled variables; adding them would ensure full reproducibility of growth conditions.
Several equipment items list only nominal accuracy (e.g., Â±1 mL) without calibration details; specifying calibration procedures would improve methodological rigor.
The preliminary experiment section demonstrates thoughtful validation of procedure and justifies equipment selection, enhancing methodological credibility.
The procedure omits the agar concentration (e.g., g agar per 100 mL water), hindering exact reproduction; include this quantitative detail.
Specify in the procedure the exact petri dish dimensions and pipette volumes for inoculation to reinforce reproducibility of the volumeâgeometry relationship.
Raw-data tables use separate tables per ingredient, making cross-comparison cumbersome; consolidating into a single table with an ingredient column would improve clarity.
Error bars appear in the graph but axis labels read âmean unitâ without specifying cm; precise labeling is needed for unambiguous data communication.
Ethical and environmental considerations are thoroughly addressed, demonstrating comprehensive awareness of biosafety and waste disposal protocols.
The comprehensive raw-data table across five conditions showcases meticulous recording; this clarity supports accurate downstream analysis.
Reporting the ANOVA p-value as â0.00â sacrifices precision; present it as p<0.001 or to three decimal places to reflect true statistical resolution.
The standard deviation calculation for one condition is shown, but the summation formula contains a typo (âdiematerâ); correct the LaTeX expression to maintain precision.
Graph 1 displays mean inhibition zones effectively but omits error bars; adding SD or SE bars would visually convey variability.
The discussion under Graph 1 confuses zinc chloride with âzinc fluorideâ; correct terminology to avoid misinterpretation of results.
Percentage uncertainty for menthol (33%) is high but not discussed; adding commentary on how this impacts confidence in almost zero inhibition results would deepen analysis.
Including a scale bar on Figure 2 would support measurement validation when interpreting inhibition zones from the image.
The limitations table omits the influence of non-active ingredients; include this specific methodological weakness to fully capture experimental constraints.
Consider critically evaluating anomalies such as the zero inhibition by menthol and linking potential mechanisms to scholarly context.
The strengths section succinctly highlights multiple trials and sterile methodology, demonstrating clear awareness of experimental robustness.
The proposed improvements are relevant but stated briefly; explaining how each change directly mitigates identified limitations would deepen the evaluation.
Future investigations suggest varying strain and temperature; also consider establishing doseâresponse curves by testing different concentrations of each active ingredient.
The reference list is extensive but citation formats vary; apply a consistent style (e.g., APA or MLA) for professional presentation.

strands.name: Methodology description

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is outlined within a broad context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
